title: High CPU / Temperature on Network Device
symptoms: CPU utilization on router/switch exceeds 80% for >5 minutes
Device temperature exceeds operating threshold (typically >60°C for routing hardware)
Routing protocols become sluggish or drop adjacencies due to CPU starvation
steps: Check CPU utilization: 'show processes cpu sorted' — identify the top consumer (interrupt vs process)
If interrupt-driven CPU: suspect a traffic storm or DoS — check 'show interfaces | inc input rate'
If process-driven: identify the process (BGP, OSPF, CEF, SNMP) causing the spike
For temperature: 'show environment temperature' — compare against threshold
Check fan status: 'show environment fan' — failed fans cause thermal shutdown
Check datacenter ambient temperature with facilities team
Inspect physical airflow: ensure no blanking panels missing, hot/cold aisle separation intact
For SNMP-caused CPU spike: reduce polling frequency or disable ifXTable polling temporarily
resolution: High CPU from traffic storm: apply rate-limiting or ACL to suppress offending traffic. High CPU from process: restart the process if safe (e.g., 'clear ip bgp' for BGP storm). Temperature: fix cooling immediately — clean air filters, check CRAC units, add temporary spot cooler. Fan failure: order replacement FRU and place device on priority watchlist.
escalation: If temperature exceeds critical threshold (>70°C) and cooling cannot be restored in 15 minutes, power down the device gracefully to prevent hardware damage. Notify Network Architect for traffic re-routing.
affected_services: Any services hosted on affected device
reference: Emircom Hardware Health Monitoring SOP — Rev 2.1
